If expressing yourself honestly would make others less comfortable with your role, should you still do it?
Should someone in a respected position be allowed to act in a way that seems less dignified but more true to themselves?
If a role demands behavior that feels emotionally exhausting, should a person be free to stop meeting that demand?
Should people be allowed to define their own version of what a role should look like, rather than accept the standard one?
If a person’s self-expression conflicts with what makes others feel they can rely on them, should self-expression come first?
Should a student be free to openly reject a role like “team leader,” “class representative,” or “family peacemaker” if that role no longer matches who they are?
If a job, club, or family expects a certain image from someone, should that person be allowed to break the image for the sake of honesty?
Should people be encouraged to keep role expectations even when those expectations pressure them to hide important parts of themselves?
If a person’s way of being naturally unsettles the role they were given, should they continue anyway?
